title: Tapping for Never Enough Hours
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the day that is already behind before it starts
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the persistent feeling that there is not enough time. The script works on the list-versus-hours gap, the anxiety that time pressure generates, and the permission to do what fits today rather than what was supposed to fit.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use at the start of a day when the list is already longer than the hours, or when the time-pressure feeling is running before you have even started.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the hours-and-list gap. The script addresses the time-anxiety that runs before the day starts, the feeling of already being behind, and the permission to work with what today actually contains.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though there is never enough time and today is already behind, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the list is longer than the day, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am tired of feeling like time is running out, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** There is not enough time today.
**Side of eye:** The list does not fit inside the hours.
**Under eye:** I started late and now I am already behind.
**Under nose:** Every time I finish something, three more things appear.
**Chin:** The anxiety about time is taking up time.
**Collarbone:** I am trying to do too much and I know it.
**Under arm:** I do not know how other people manage their days.
**Top of head:** The not-enough feeling starts before the day does.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I can only do what fits in today.
**Side of eye:** The list will still be there tomorrow. So will I.
**Under eye:** Done enough is done.
**Under nose:** I do not need to empty the list. I need to pick the three things that actually matter today.
**Chin:** Moving steadily does more than racing.
**Collarbone:** Time pressure is a feeling. It can change.
**Under arm:** I have done something. Something always counts.
**Top of head:** I have enough time for what actually needs doing today.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
